seoTitle: How to Create a Calendar View in Airtable
metaDescription: Build an interactive calendar in Airtable by mapping date fields to a Calendar view, dragging cards to update dates, and sharing a live link.
intro: Airtable's Calendar view turns your date fields into a visual, drag-and-drop schedule, which is handy for tracking editorial calendars or project timelines. This guide covers setting up date fields, creating the calendar view, and sharing it with your team.

steps.heading: Set up your date fields
steps.body: In your grid table, make sure you have date fields, such as start times or deadlines, ready to plot.

steps.heading: Open the Views sidebar
steps.body: Navigate to the bottom left Views sidebar and select the Calendar layout option.

steps.heading: Name your calendar
steps.body: Choose a name and decide whether it should be a collaborative or personal view.

steps.heading: Map your date fields
steps.body: Select the date columns you want to use to plot your records across the visual calendar.

steps.heading: Adjust and share
steps.body: Drag and drop calendar cards to update dates directly, apply color-coding rules, and generate a shareable calendar link.

faq.question: Does dragging a card on the calendar actually update the underlying data?
faq.answer: Yes, moving a card in the Calendar view updates the corresponding date field in your base's underlying table.

faq.question: Can I have multiple calendar views for the same table?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create several Calendar views mapped to different date fields or filtered to show different subsets of records.

faq.question: Is the shared calendar link editable by outside viewers?
faq.answer: That depends on the sharing permissions you set, since you can configure a link to be view-only or allow editing.

faq.question: What if a record doesn't have a date filled in yet?
faq.answer: Records without a value in the mapped date field typically won't appear on the calendar until a date is added.
